name: BlackRock
desc: World's largest asset manager. Funds both parties through hundreds of employee PAC contributions. Top recipient: Senate Banking Committee members.

name: Koch Industries
desc: Second-largest private company in the US. Overwhelmingly Republican donor. Funds the Club for Growth, Americans for Prosperity, and ALEC. Opposes climate regulation and minimum wage.

name: ExxonMobil
desc: Largest US oil company. Spent decades funding climate denial. PAC donates overwhelmingly to Republicans on energy committees. Lobbied against Paris Agreement withdrawal.

name: Comcast / NBCUniversal
desc: Largest US cable company and major media conglomerate. Bipartisan donor focused on telecom regulation and net neutrality. Lobbied against FCC broadband oversight.

name: Alphabet / Google
desc: Dominates search, digital advertising, and AI. Bipartisan donor. Lobbied heavily against antitrust legislation. Employees donate predominantly Democratic but corporate PAC is balanced.

name: AT&T
desc: Second-largest US telecom. Opposes net neutrality, supports telecom deregulation. Major donor across party lines on Commerce and Judiciary committees.

name: Lockheed Martin
desc: Largest US defense contractor. F-35, missile systems, satellite tech. Donates heavily to Armed Services and Defense Appropriations committee members of both parties.

name: Raytheon / RTX
desc: Major defense contractor making missiles, radar, and jet engines. Patriot missile systems manufacturer. Heavily bipartisan — donates to all Armed Services members.

name: Goldman Sachs
desc: Investment bank and financial services giant. Virtually the definition of Wall Street influence. Top donor to Senate Banking and Finance committee members of both parties.

name: Microsoft
desc: Largest software company. Bipartisan tech donor. Lobbied for immigration reform (H-1B visas), AI regulation, and cybersecurity policy. Acquired Activision Blizzard in $69B deal requiring regulatory approval.

name: AIPAC (American Israel Public Affairs Committee)
desc: Most powerful foreign policy lobby in the US. Ran primary challenges against progressive Democrats in 2024. United Democracy Project (AIPAC Super PAC) spent $100M+ targeting races.

name: Pfizer
desc: World's largest pharmaceutical company. COVID vaccine maker. Bipartisan donor. Lobbied against Medicare drug price negotiation in Inflation Reduction Act. Funds both parties' health committee members.

name: Meta / Facebook
desc: Social media giant facing antitrust, privacy, and content moderation scrutiny. Shifted toward Republicans under Zuckerberg post-2024. Lobbied against EU regulations and Section 230 changes.

name: Boeing
desc: Major defense contractor and commercial aircraft manufacturer. Lobbied heavily after 737 MAX crisis. Donations targeted at Armed Services and Transportation committee members.

name: JPMorgan Chase
desc: Largest US bank. CEO Jamie Dimon has positioned JPMorgan as bipartisan. Major donor to Senate Banking Committee members. Opposed the Basel III capital rules.

name: Amazon
desc: E-commerce and cloud computing giant. Largest private employer. Lobbied against unionization, for antitrust exemptions, and on AI/cloud policy. Amazon Web Services has massive government contracts.

name: Chevron
desc: Second-largest US oil company. Consistently Republican donor. Lobbied against climate legislation and for expanded drilling rights. Benefited from Chevron Deference Supreme Court ruling reversal.

name: Cigna / Evernorth
desc: Major health insurer. Lobbied against Medicare for All, drug pricing reform, and ACA expansion. Profits from the current insurance system. Heavily funds Health committee members.

name: UnitedHealth Group
desc: Largest US health insurer. Among the most profitable companies in America. Opposed Medicare negotiation. CEO murder triggered national discussion about healthcare industry practices.

name: Northrop Grumman
desc: Major defense contractor. B-21 Raider stealth bomber, nuclear weapons systems, space systems. Bipartisan Armed Services donor. Major contracts across all military branches.
